episodes.intent: Initial project exploration — understand codebase layout, dependencies, and test expectations
episodes.what_it_did: Ran bash to inspect repo layout; read package.json (deps: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.0.0, zod ^3.22.0; devDeps: typescript, vitest); read src/index.ts stub and tests/index.test.ts (asserts listTools() returns tool with properties.a/b and listResources() returns config resource at URI config://app/settings)
episodes.outcome: TypeScript MCP server project found with stub createServer(); deps confirmed including SDK ^1.0.0; two tests assert calculate_sum tool schema has properties.a and properties.b, and config resource has correct name and URI

episodes.intent: Investigate the MCP SDK's tool() / registerTool() API signatures and Zod schema integration via grep/read
episodes.what_it_did: Repeatedly grepped the entire MCP SDK source for every mention of tool(), registerTool(), resource() across all files; saved full captures to ~/memory/sdk_tool_api.txt; also grepped separately for resource(); saved those captures to ~/memory/resource_api.txt; re-read both memory files back to reconstruct full context in working memory
episodes.outcome: SDK exposes both tool()/registerTool() (with multiple overloads each) and resource()/registerResource(); confirmed via grep captures in ~/memory/sdk_tool_api.txt and ~/memory/resource_api.txt that were re-read to validate findings

episodes.intent: Investigate the MCP SDK's resource() / registerResource() API — signature, callback interface, and ReadResourceResult structure
episodes.what_it_did: Grepped _registeredResources usage in mcp.js to trace resource read-request handling; traced through SDK source to see how resource callbacks are invoked; searched server-side example files (none found); fell back to reading .d.ts type declarations to resolve ReadResourceCallback type, ReadResourceResult type, TextResourceContents interface, BlobResourceContents interface, and ResourceContents base interface
episodes.outcome: Discovered resource() takes (name, uri, readCallback) where callback receives (uri, extra) and must return { contents: [{ uri, mimeType?, text }] }; ReadResourceResult extends Result with a contents array of (TextResourceContents | BlobResourceContents)

episodes.intent: Diagnose why listTools/listResources fail after initial implementation attempt
episodes.what_it_did: Read index.ts from working memory to review current state; ran grep -Rn on the SDK source for listTools/listResources (found they are MCP protocol request handlers set via setRequestHandler in setToolRequestHandlers/setResourceRequestHandlers, not public instance methods); examined McpServer class public members; inspected internal dicts _registeredTools and _registeredResources to see if usable as backing data
episodes.outcome: Confirmed McpServer has no public listTools/listResources methods — only MCP protocol request handlers triggered during client-server communication; discovered _registeredTools/_registeredResources internal dicts contain the registered definitions but tests call them as direct methods

episodes.intent: Fix the missing listTools/listResources by adding custom accessor methods on the server instance
episodes.what_it_did: Wrote updated index.ts from working memory to replace the stub createServer() with implementation that registers calculate_sum tool and config resource, and adds listTools()/listResources() wrapper methods accessing _registeredTools and _registeredResources
episodes.outcome: After editing index.ts, tests ran again: resource test now passes (config resource correctly registered at expected URI), but tool test still fails with expected{...}toHaveProperty('properties') error indicating inputSchema is a ZodObject rather than plain JSON-schema with properties

episodes.intent: Fix the tool inputSchema conversion — convert the Zod schema to a proper JSON-schema object with properties using zod-to-json-schema
episodes.what_it_did: Read sdk's zod-compat.js to understand normalizeObjectSchema behavior; confirmed zod-to-json-schema package is available in the project; wrote new index.ts importing zod-to-json-schema and passing it to the listTools() implementation for converting each tool's inputSchema
episodes.outcome: Tests partially improved — resource test still passes; tool test still fails with same assertion error suggesting the conversion may not be wired up correctly or another issue exists

episodes.intent: Debug why zod-to-json-schema isn't producing the expected output — inspect _createRegisteredTool internals and run ad-hoc diagnostics on actual registered-tool objects
episodes.what_it_did: Read mcp.js around _createRegisteredTool to understand stored structure per tool entry; attempted node -e debug script (hit tsx --loader deprecation warning, switched to --import flag), checked standalone zodToJsonSchema output (correct — produces top-level properties), inspected actual tool.inputSchema in _registeredTools (confirmed ZodObject with ._def.typeName='ZodObject' and .shape containing field schemas), then called zodToJsonSchema on real inputSchema with name option and got back $ref-wrapped result with nested definitions
episodes.outcome: Confirmed the problem: calling zodToJsonSchema(tool.inputSchema, { name }) wraps output in '$ref':'#/definitions/<name>' instead of placing properties at root; without the name argument the conversion yields clean {type:'object',properties:{a,b}} at top level

episodes.intent: Apply final fix — remove name option from zodToJsonSchema so inputSchema lands with top-level properties — then run tests one last time
episodes.what_it_did: Edited index.ts in place to change zodToJsonSchema call from {name: 'calculate_sum'} to omit the name argument entirely, then ran npm test
episodes.outcome: All tests pass — vitest reports 'tests/index.test.ts (2 tests) 5ms' with '2 passed (2)', confirming both calculate_sum tool schema (now with proper top-level properties) and config resource registration satisfy their assertions
